text: Excuse me sir, is this your Rolls Royce? Yeah. How did you get rich? How did I get rich? Yeah, what line of business for you? When I go all over the world, I actually grew up very, very poor. What were you saying? I grew up in Indiana, very, very poor. I didn't like being poor, so I just outworked everybody. What line of business did you get into? Well, I first started running companies, and I started buying companies. So like the private equity game? Yes. What was the number one business book that changed your life? I never read a business book. You never read a business book? No, I was born with it in my DNA. Do you think everybody spilled for entrepreneurship though? There's 8 billion people in the world. There's only 5,000 people in the world that have $500 million of net worth. You go to where you put them? Are you one of those people? Absolutely. And I believe God sends 8 billion people down the line. Like in an automobile industry, we all get parts. Who is your God? Jesus Christ. So you're a Christian? Absolutely. How did you know God was real? How did you know Jesus was real? I grew up in a family that didn't have a car, couldn't pay the rent. And so there's a story they tell. I was 12 years old coming from the Lillid Park. I got two of my friends. We were very, very poor. And what happened is Walt Disney was building Disney World in Anaheim. I never talked about how they're going to save their money and go to Disney World. And my friends asked me, why was I not speaking? And I said, I'm listening. And they said, you want to go to Disney World? And I said, no, I want to own it. I wasn't thinking about the roller coaster at 12 years old. I was thinking about all those people standing in line and I'm in this little booth like the movie theater taking in the money. And that's because when I got sent down the line, God gave me skills that other people didn't have. How do you know? I'm 82. You're not 82. I am. Are you lied to me? No, I'm not. I haven't lied to you at all. I'm 82 years old. How do you look like this at 82? I would have thought maybe 60. My dad had a story about that. He was 20 years older than my mother. He said, Mr. Ewing, how do you look so good? My dad said, son, there's five reasons. I don't smoke, drink, cause, do drugs, associate with wild women. He said, I've only had one really, really bad habit in my life. He said, son, I lie like hell. That's original from my father. If me and you died tomorrow and you had one more message to leave with the younger generation, what would that be? Never quit. You know why? Everybody can do that. All eight billion people have one skill that's in common. They all can quit. Never give up. You got amazing advice, sir. I connect with all the billionaires at any of you. Could I get your contact? I can give you my phone number. I don't do email.
